steps_text.text: Secure Cloud Access with CyberArk and WizIn this tour, we're going to explore how to detect standing privileges and move to zero standing privileges. Let's go!Made with  Storylane
Let's begin our journey to zero standing privileges in the Wiz platform by identifying which standing privileges exist in our multi-cloud environment. Next
Navigate to the relevant access control area to check for misconfigurations or excessive access...Next
🚨Risk Identified!Thanks to Wiz's deep visibility, when you scroll down you can see several user accounts and groups with excessive privileges.Next
Let's dive into this Identity Center Group with excessive privileges in the AWS console.
We can see here that the data scientist role associated with the group BD Tech is flagged as a risk for excessive privileges. Time to secure it with CyberArk Secure Cloud Access.Next

Leveraging the CyberArk and Wiz integration, you can now send this issue directly to the CyberArk platform to secure these identities by setting up access policies with CyberArk Secure Cloud Access.Run the action to see the magic ✨
⚡Action executedNow that the issue has been sent for remediation, CyberArk identity security controls will be applied to any user in the BD Tech group when accessing the "Data Scientist" role.Next
Did it work, though?Let's check. Next
We've logged in as a user from the BD Tech Group (after completing strong multi-factor authentication, of course).
Now we'll connect to AWS Identity Center via Secure Cloud Access.
✅ Session control✅ Session monitoring✅ Session protectionActivating...
Locate the permission set for which the secure cloud access policy was recently created by Wiz. Then, hover and click "connect".
By clicking connect, the data scientist role will be dynamically provisioned and deprovisioned upon this connection, ensuring zero standing privileges when connected to the AWS Identity Center.Next

First, we will SELL a PUT. We will keep it closest to 0.3 Delta which is at a 24200 Strike.

Now, we will BUY a PUT. We will buy closest to 0.1 Delta which is at a 23300 Strike.

steps_text.text: Olá!Para darmos início ao nosso tour, vamos te apresentar os aplicativos Pontotel e em seguida você poderá conferir detalhes da nossa versão web também! Acompanhe até o fim para entender melhor cada um deles.PRÓXIMOMade with  Storylane

Aplicativo BatePontoO app BatePonto é destinado aos colaboradores! Sua função é realizar a marcação de ponto via celular de qualquer lugar, podendo ser realizada mesmo sem acesso a rede móvel! Veja como funciona:PRÓXIMO

Um painel ficará disponível para que o colaborador possa selecionar qual o tipo de ponto deseja registrar: entrada, pausa, retorno ou saída. Pontos já registrados ficarão sinalizados na cor cinza, para que o colaborador não selecione a opção errada, sendo possível também visualizar de forma rápida se algum ponto foi esquecido ao longo do dia.PRÓXIMO

Ao escolher o ponto desejado, a câmera do celular será acionada para que uma foto em tempo real seja registrada, desta forma sendo possível realizar o reconhecimento facial com base no banco de dados registrado.PRÓXIMO

Pronto!Assim que reconhecido a face do colaborador, a confirmação de identificação e registro do ponto são realizados de forma automatizada. O usuário tem a opção de realizar o download de seu comprovante de ponto diretamente no telefone ou pode apenas finalizar.PRÓXIMO

Aplicativo Gestão, + Estratégia pro seu RHEssa é apenas uma das funções do nosso aplicativo para registro de ponto. Ficou curioso e quer conhecer todos os processos que podemos descomplicar na sua rotina com o aplicativo Gestão Pontotel? Descubra como seu RH pode se tornar mais estratégico, clicando no botão.QUERO TORNAR MEU RH MAIS ESTRATÉGICOVER MAIS DO TOUR

Ao acessar o aplicativo você poderá ter um resumo de panorama geral do momento atual de sua equipe. É possível visualizar quantos colaboradores estão trabalhando, necessitam ainda iniciar suas atividades ou estão ausentes.PRÓXIMO

Ao selecionar o comando desejado é possível observar um campo para visualização individual por funcionário, indicando qual em específico está dentro do filtro.Ao visualizar os colaboradores trabalhando, um resumo de quantos estão realizando horas extras ou em pausa também fica disponível para o gestor.PRÓXIMO
Na opção “ausentes” um panorama da quantidade de funcionários em: férias, dispensas, afastamentos, faltas, folgas ou com jornadas já encerradas, fica disponível para visualização.PRÓXIMO
Ao alterar a visualização para “período”, é possível ver informações do mês vigente ou anteriores. Um resumo de eventos da equipe também fica disponível, podendo ser administrado pelo gestor.PRÓXIMO
É muito simples lançar uma dispensa pela Pontotel! Primeiro, clique em "Lançamentos" no menu.PRÓXIMO
Selecione o colaborador que deseja lançar a dispensa.PRÓXIMO
Em seguida, escolha qual o motivo da dispensa do colaborador.PRÓXIMO
Após, selecione o período, ou seja, qual a data da devida dispensa. PRÓXIMO
Uma tela para informações relacionadas aos horários da dispensa ficará disponível, basta fazer os ajustes conforme as informações de jornada do colaborador.PRÓXIMO

Inclua informações a respeito de atestados médicos em caso de dispensas para este fim. É possível anexar documentos e preencher de forma manual campos específicos. Após concluir, clique em “confirmar”.PRÓXIMO
Dispensa finalizada! O evento será adicionado de forma automática diretamente na folha do colaborador.PRÓXIMO

Agora ficou difícil continuar sem saber mais do nosso aplicativo Gestão, não é?O app da Pontotel é dedicado exclusivamente para tornar mais assertivo e estratégico o trabalho dos gestores e do RH. E ainda tem mais…Você ainda contará com uma versão Web, onde ficam disponíveis funcionalidades avançadas e exclusivas para gerenciamento de equipes, jornada e mais. Veja mais detalhes:QUERO UMA DEMONSTRAÇÃO DO APP GESTÃOVER MAIS DO TOUR

Gestão em tempo realUma maneira de gerenciar de forma segura e completa todas as atualizações diárias da jornada da sua equipe. Com essa funcionalidade, você terá dados confiáveis para otimizar a gestão de jornada dos seus colaboradores na palma da sua mão.QUERO TER INFORMAÇÕES EM TEMPO REALCONTINUAR O TOUR
Em "ausentes", é possível verificar a quantidade de funcionários que estão ausentes e o motivo - que pode ser férias, dispensas, afastamentos, faltas, jornada encerrada ou folgas.PRÓXIMO
Na aba “trabalhando” consulte quantos funcionários já iniciariam sua jornada e quantos estão fazendo hora extra ou estão prestes a estender o limite diário de sua jornada.PROXIMO
Na aba “em atraso ou a iniciar” é possível ainda verificar quantos colaboradores estão atrasados ou ainda irão iniciar sua jornada dentro do horário previsto.PRÓXIMO

+ Detalhes na folha de pontoUma visão da folha de ponto completa para analisar horários de entrada, pausa, retorno e saída. E seu RH ainda conta com ajustes em massa ou individuais facilitados em poucos cliques, além do resumo otimizado dos apontamentos do dia.DESEJO UMA FOLHA DE PONTO OTIMIZADACONTINUAR O TOUR
Na verificação de folhas do sistema Pontotel, você consegue verificar e realizar ajustes em poucos cliques!PRÓXIMO
Para ajustar marcações do ponto, basta adicionar o horário correto e selecionar o motivo da correção.PRÓXIMO
Desta forma, todos os apontamentos são gerados automaticamente.PRÓXIMO

Verificar FolhasCom a função de verificação de folhas, você terá mais tranquilidade, sabendo que tudo está em ordem sem perder tempo. Com ele o RH pode corrigir rapidamente as inconsistências nos registros de ponto de vários colaboradores.QUERO SABER MAIS SOBRE ESSA FUNÇÃOCONTINUAR O TOUR
Selecione o colaborador que deseja verificar a folha de ponto ou selecione vários ao mesmo tempo.PRÓXIMO
Ao selecionar vários colaboradores, você terá acesso aos pontos inconsistentes, podendo fazer correções individuais.PRÓXIMO
Também é possível conferir quantos pontos inconsistentes os colaboradores totais selecionados possuem e desta forma corrigi-los de forma automática.PRÓXIMO
Pronto! Ao selecionar correção automática, os pontos inconsistentes serão ajustados, de forma fácil e rápida.PRÓXIMO

Travamento de folhaA função travamento é realizada após gestores e colaboradores verificarem as folhas. Com ela é possível impedir alterações, reduzindo riscos e erros no processo de fechamento de folha. Mais segurança no tratamento de ponto e fechamento de folha de pagamento!PRECISO DESSA FUNÇÃOCONTINUAR O TOUR

Para realizar o travamento, basta filtrar por local de trabalho ou empregado e em seguida escolher o mês e ano que deseja travar a folha de ponto. É possível escolher mais de um colaborador de uma vez.PRÓXIMO
Ao selecionar mais de uma opção de visualização, todos os colaboradores dentro do filtro selecionado irão ficar visível.PRÓXIMO

Em seguida, basta selecionar a opção de travamento desejada: pode-se travar de forma unilateral, onde apenas o RH ou gestores têm acesso a esta opção, ou iniciar travamento, onde é feito de forma conjunta com o colaborador.PRÓXIMO

Painel de acompanhamentoCom essa visão é possível acompanhar apontamentos em tempo real do time. E através de gráficos, suas análises serão mais precisas e descomplicadas, permitindo uma ampla visão gerencial do cenário atual.QUERO TER MAIS VISÃO DO MEU RHCONTINUAR O TOUR
É possível filtrar por datas, local de trabalho e qual tipo de apontamento deseja visualizar, podendo escolher mais de um ao mesmo tempo.PRÓXIMO
Cada apontamento é representado por uma cor específica e elas aparecerão no gráfico conforme forem uma realidade da equipe.PRÓXIMO

Gestão de escalaCom essa funcionalidade, o controle de alocação dos esforços se torna mais preciso e seguro. Você terá certeza onde cada colaborador está alocado filtrando por datas, por mês ou semana.PRECISO DESSA VISÃO DE ESCALACONTINUAR O TOUR
Basta filtrar local de trabalho ou colaborador, período e qual visualização deseja.PRÓXIMO

classification.summary: This 40-step demo presents the Pontotel time-tracking and HR management system by walking through multiple features across two mobile apps and a web platform. While comprehensive in feature coverage, the demo lacks a clear connecting narrative thread, jumping between unrelated functionalities without establishing a single compelling outcome or tying features to specific business value.
strengths: Logical UI flow within individual features (e.g., the 6-step dispensa/exemption process is sequenced well)
Covers multiple user personas (employees via BatePonto app, managers/HR via Gestão app)

weaknesses: Massive feature dump: 40 steps covering 8+ distinct features with no overarching narrative connecting them
No clear problem statement or opening hook explaining why Pontotel matters
Generic benefit language: 'more strategic,' 'easier,' 'safer' without specific metrics or outcomes
Zero credibility proof: no numbers, no customer testimonials, no before/after comparisons, no real data shown

Early steps show employee time-clock features (Steps 2-5), then shifts to manager dashboard overview (Steps 7-10), then into administrative tasks like dispensas (Steps 11-17). Mid-demo, vague transition to 'more features' (Step 18). Then 8+ separate feature modules (real-time management, timesheet details, sheet verification, lock functionality, analytics, shift management) are introduced sequentially with no climax or resolution. Demo ends abruptly after shift management without summarizing value or next steps.
classification.persona_targeting: Partially multi-persona (employee vs. manager/HR) but no differentiation in messaging. Both audiences see the same feature-focused descriptions without acknowledging their distinct success metrics. Employee using BatePonto cares about speed and simplicity; manager using Gestão cares about visibility and compliance. These needs are never explicitly addressed.
classification.proof_elements: None present. No metrics (e.g., '90% reduction in timesheet errors'), no customer logos, no testimonials, no before/after comparisons, no realistic data examples. Dashboard screenshots show clean UI but no evidence of real-world performance or customer success.
classification.recommendation: Restructure the demo around a single, measurable business outcome (e.g., 'Reduce timesheet processing time by 80%'). Open with a relatable pain point for the primary persona (e.g., HR manager losing 10 hours/week on manual corrections). Feature only 4-5 core capabilities that directly ladder up to that outcome. Compress to 8-12 steps. Add one customer testimonial or specific metric (e.g., 'Client ABC processes payroll 3 days faster'). Create separate branching paths for Employee vs. Manager personas with role-specific language and success metrics.

Once the initial scan is complete, you will be able to see all discovered devices and they are displayed in a Device List.Next
Click here to take a look at the information Domotz uncovers about a device when it is discovered.

Unlike competitors, Domotz performs a Layer-2 scan (on top of the Layer-3 scan) to more accurately identify all devices and surface more information about the device such as device type, make, model, and more. This also allows to uniquely identify physical devices which might change IP addresses.Next

Domotz displays offline devices in red and shares when the device was last connected.Next

Click Alerts to get started.

steps_text.text: Let's begin by finding a client's Risk NumberThis Risk Assessment is built to quantify an investor's risk tolerance in less than 5 minutes. And the beauty of it lies within its adaptive, dynamic format. Using real dollar amounts, each question builds off the last to make a truly interactive experience.1/6Next
First, your client will select up to three financial goals. There aren't any right or wrong answers; these selections are just here to help guide the conversation between you and your clients.2/6Next

Hypotheticals that aren’t tied to real, relevant dollar amounts won’t get to the heart of someone’s risk tolerance.That's why we use real dollar amounts and have each question build off the last to create a truly interactive experience.3/6Next

Did you know that human beings are 2.5x more concerned about avoiding losses than they are about potential gains?As your clients move this slider from left to right, keep an eye on those dollar amounts on the left side of your screen and think about the maximum dollar amount they would be willing to risk in comparison to how much they could gain over the next 6-month period. 4/6Next

Next, your client will be met with a series of questions that will put their comfort zone to the test.Think of each question separately. Which would help them sleep better at night, Option A or Option B?5/6Next
After completing the questionnaire, Nitrogen will crunch all of the data and generate your client's Risk Number! 🎉6/6Next Module

steps_text.text: Welcome to the TOS Discovery Interactive DemoWe will learn how TOS Discovery, automatically discovers and onboards all your Cisco routers/switches, keeping topology always accurate and up-to-date.Let's get started!
Here we can see a list of Cisco devices, including firewalls, routers and switches.All these devices have been added manually.Next
Click Map to open Tufin's interactive map.
We are now going to run the Path Analysis tool, to analyse the traffic between two points in our network.Click Find path.

Here we can see an incomplete network connection, with missing monitored devices in Tufin, affecting network troubleshooting as well as the firewall change process.Before TOS Discovery, we would have to investigate manually what is missing from the topology map, which can take hours per device….This is exactly where TOS Discovery comes in.Next
Using the highlighted network range, where the Cisco devices reside, and the SNMP credentials entered, we will now discover devices using these parameters.Click Discover.
We can see that 2 devices have been discovered.Next
Let's go ahead and view the devices in the Device Dashboard.Clicking Device.

On the left side of the screen we can see the two routers that were added.In real environments there can be thousands of network devices. We are now going to look at a way to organize and groups these devices. Click Auto Grouping.
Now that the devices have been grouped by IP pattern, naming convention etc. we are going to go ahead and onboard these devices to Tufin.Click TOS Discovery.
The entire export process from discovery, all the way through to onboarding, can be fully automated by enabling the Automatic Export button.Next
For the purposes of this demo, we are going to view the export process manually.Click Manual Export.
These two devices are the missing routers from the topology map! TOS Discovery has done it's job!Let's go ahead and export these devices to TOS.Click Export to TOS.
Here we can see that the two devices have been exported to TOS.Next
Back in TOS, we can see the missing devices have been automatically added and are now monitored.Next
Click Map, so we can rerun the Path Analysis query, with the missing devices now included.
Click Find path.

We can see the path is now complete, including the missing devices.The accurate reliable topology has helped with our troubleshooting scenario and will also accelerate change automation, compliance and vulnerability mitigation within TOS.Next
Ready to Make an Impact?Thanks for exploring! Ready to see how this can work for you? Our team is here to help you get started.Get a demo

scriptrunner.storylane.io/demo/lmjrrcnsd.name: ScriptRunner

steps_text.text: Chapter hub with 5 chapters: 1Bring Order to Your Automatio, 2Enforce Security Across Every, 3Automate the RoutineEmpower n, 4Unlock Expert Knowledge Insta, 5Replace Legacy Solutions with

Centralize your automation with structured and policy-driven actionsScriptRunner lets you define what runs, where, and how - with full visibility through live monitoring and detailed execution reports. Everything is organized, secure, and easy to control.Define Actions to bundle scripts, targets, credentials, and input parameters.Apply policy-based configuration to control execution conditions and access.Connect scripts to queries, web service calls, and external systemsMonitor execution in real

Let's take a look at how actions work in ScriptRunner.Actions are the core building blocks of ScriptRunner - this is where everything comes together.We'll start by navigating to the Actions section.Next

An Action combines all the key components you need to run a script:The script itself - the code you want to executeThe target - where the script will runThe credentials - the permissions used to execute the scriptAny schedules you want to assignThe access policies that control who can use it...and much more.This is the central place where everything is configured.Next
Now, let's see how an action actually works.We'll open an existing action that has been already createdNext
When we create an action, ScriptRunner automatically generates a form based on the script's input parameters.This form is built from the script argument block and it’s ready to use right away.Next

Here, we can enter values like Given Name, Surname, and eMail - these are simple text fields.Behind the scenes, regular expressions are used to validate the input format, ensuring that data is entered correctly.Next
Now let’s look at the Department field.This one is a query type parameter - it shows a dropdown that dynamically queries organizational units in real time.Next
When we open the dropdown, we can see that it pulls in values like organizational units.This dropdown is powered by a query that’s defined separately under the Queries section in ScriptRunner.Next
Once we’ve filled in the form, we can simply click Run to execute the action.This is one of the easiest ways to trigger a PowerShell script directly from the ScriptRunner portal.

Once we click Run, a pop-up appears showing the live execution logs for this action.You can observe the progress here in real time.But you don’t need to wait on this screen - we can head over to Live Monitor to continue tracking the execution.Next
In the Live Monitor, we see a list of all currently running scripts.This gives us real-time visibility into what's being executed.Next
Once a script finishes, its results will be available under the Reports section.We’ll explore Reports in more detail in another chapter.Next
Now that we've seen the use of the action, let's explore how it was created.We'll walk through the key steps that make an action in ScriptRunner.First, we go to Configuration > Scripts
To create a new script, you'd normally click Create – but we'll cover the different ways to create scripts in a separate chapter.Next
For now, let’s open a pre-created script to understand how it works.
Here in the Code Editor, we can see the actual PowerShell code.The param block is key - it's what ScriptRunner uses to automatically generate the input form we saw earlier in the action.Next

You can see parameters like Name, Surname, and Email, each with built-in validation using regular expressions.There’s also a query type parameter for department and a PowerShell credential parameter.This is the first step in creating an action - defining your script and its parameters.Next
Next, let’s look at how to configure the query behind that Department dropdown.We go to Configuration > Queries and click Create.

ScriptRunner supports multiple types of queries – from static lists and files to dynamic queries using PowerShell scripts.For example, you can fetch values from a database, Active Directory, or any external system.These queries allow your input forms to populate dropdowns or lists dynamically.Next
Now let’s define where the script will run – we do that under Configuration > Targets.

A Target is essentially a configuration file that defines the execution environment – the system where your script will run.ScriptRunner supports different types of targets like:Local machinePowerShell RemotingMicrosoft 365 and Azure... moreEach target contains all the necessary connection settings and credentials to run scripts securely and reliably.Next
Cloud-based targets like Microsoft 365, Teams, Azure AD, and Graph API come built-in.You simply select the service, add the credentials, and that’s it – ScriptRunner handles the rest.Next

Now let’s add the credentials that your scripts and targets can use.These credentials are securely stored in ScriptRunner and can be injected into scripts or linked to specific targets and actions.Just navigate to Configuration > Credentials, click Create

ScriptRunner supports various credential types, including:External vaults via Password ServerWindows credential storeWinRM client certificatesSSH keysPowerShell Secret Management Choose the method that fits your environment — whether you're storing secrets locally or using a centralized vault.Next

Now that we’ve created all the key components - Script, Query, Target, and Credentials - we can put everything together by creating an Action.We click Actions > Create, and here’s where it all connects.
First, define the Display Name - this is the name of the action
Go to the Script section – browse the script library and select the script you’ve already created
Click on your script
If the param is query, click Link Query and select the query
If the param is credential, click Link Credential and choose a credential
Select a Credential from the ones you created
In the Target section, select the target environment where the script should run
Click on "Select targets"
Select a Target from the ones you created

And of course, there are many additional settings you can configure - like schedules, access policies, notifications, and more.You can check the full list of options in the ScriptRunner documentation.Next
Once everything is configured, you can view or update it anytime from the Actions section.Next

Security That Scales With Your AutomationStay in control as you automate. ScriptRunner provides built-in enterprise-grade tools to secure sensitive data, track usage, and meet compliance needs - no matter how complex your environment gets.Manage credentials securely with Password Server integrationTrack every action with detailed audit logsMonitor usage with execution statistics and change logsGet a complete picture with reporting and compliance insightsExport reports for governance, compliance,

ScriptRunner supports secure credential handling across your automation landscape.Credentials can be safely injected into scripts, assigned to targets, or used in param blocks.Let’s walk through it.Next

steps_text.text: Atlas Discoveryand Certificate ManagementDiscover and manage all of your SSL/TLS certificates quickly and easily using the built-in tools available within our Atlas Digital Identity platform.Get StartedMade with  Storylane

Our certificate discovery and management features take the pain and resource tax out of manually keeping track of your SSL/TLS certificates, allowing you to run searches and check certificate inventories. These searches even include certificates issued from other Certificate Authorities, so that you only need to check using one tool. You can then use the same tool to request, view, renew, reissue and revoke certificates quickly and easily.Next
Atlas Discovery is available to any organization. Simply contact GlobalSign to create an  account, or log in with your existing credentials.Next
This is the main Atlas dashboard providing helpful reminders in the main section to prompt you to complete any outstanding tasks.We will click on "Discovery" in the navigation to continue.Next
Within the Discovery dropdown, you have the option to view the Dashboard, Certificate Inventory, Scan Profiles and your Inventory Filters.Next

Selecting "Dashboard" will show the current state of your certificate inventory from a previous scan. If you have not run a scan before, it will prompt you to run a scan to populate the dashboard and your inventory. Next

The Dashboard will provide a summary of your inventory. Here, on the left, you can see there are two certificates that have already expired, and 1 that is going to expire in the next 7 days so you can take action.On the right, the chart shows how many certificates have been issued, and from which Certificate Authority.Next
Under "Inventory", you can review all of the details of each certificate that has been discovered and is monitored. Next
Selecting a specific certificate will provide you with in-depth information regarding the certificate. Next
Within this view, you also have the option to download the certificate file. You also have the option to archive or delete the certificate from your inventory if required.Next
The "Inventory" dropdown shows all discovered certificates which can be downloaded as a .csv file if required. You can also manually upload certificates.Next
To populate your inventory, you can create a Scan Profile.Next

Creating a new scan profile is quick and easy.You need to add the domain you wish to scan and select whether it is external public scan, or via an agent for internal private servers.Then add the domain you wish to scan.Next
Once the scan is created, the status will be updated in the scan profile list.Next
Your inventory may contain hundreds of certificates, but you have the ability to create and apply a filter to target the certificates you are looking for.Next
You can define your filter using single or multiple rules which may be useful for granularity.Next
Once the filter is created and applied, you can then view those specific certificates without having to scroll through a long list.Next
Start Your Certificate Discovery JourneyContact us to enhance your Certificate Lifecycle Management strategy by taking advantage of this service.Talk To Us

steps_text.text: Chapter hub with 3 chapters: 1Tab 1 - Inherent Risk0/27, 2Tab 2 - Supplier Engagement0/, 3Tab 3 - Reports0/25
Welcome to the Modern Slavery ModuleInherent Risk simplifies supplier imports, offering real-time feedback along with detailed insights into country and industry risks.Continue
Click on this tab to see the Inherent Risk and upload suppliersNext
Suppliers is the total amount of suppliers linked or loaded under the member's profile.Next
Risk Assessed is the percentage of suppliers who have been inherently risk-assessed based on the country and industry assigned to their supplier profile.Next

Uncategorised = This figure shows suppliers missing country or industry data, so an ISRI risk rating cannot be calculated.Very Low = Suppliers with minimal country and industry risk, averaging to a ‘Very Low’ ISRI rating.Low = Suppliers with low combined country and industry risk, resulting in a ‘Low’ ISRI rating.Medium = Suppliers with moderate country and industry risk, leading to a ‘Medium’ ISRI rating.High = Suppliers with significant country and industry risk, resulting in a ‘High’ ISRI rat
Clicking on any ISRI rating figure filters the dashboard to display supplier profiles with that specific risk rating.Next

Click on Add Filter to access additional filters such as Group, Country, Industry, Rating or Engagement Status.If you select a filter, it will apply across all 3 tabs in the Modern Slavery Module until removed.Next
The Average Supplier Risk chart allows you to see the overall ISRI Risk Rating for all assessed suppliers.Next
You can quickly add a single supplier or import a bulk list of suppliers by clicking on Add Supplier.Next
To add a single supplier, click Add Record.To upload a bulk list of suppliers, click Add File.Next
Use the Reset button to correct a wrong selection of Add Record or Add File and reselect the correct option.Next

To create a new supplier record, select Add Record, then enter the company name and business identifier. Choose the industry and country from the drop-down options.Note: To generate an ISRI Rating, both industry and country must be selected.Next

When entering individual supplier data or completing the initial upload, the system will initially check only the supplier name.After clicking Import, the system will then cross-check the identifier information, prompting you to address any additional errors to avoid duplication.Next

When you click Add File to perform a bulk upload, ensure your file includes the supplier name, identifier, industry, and country. You can also upload contact details at this stage using the Advanced functions.Next
If the column titles in your spreadsheet don't match the existing ones, you can use the title drop-downs to choose the correct titles.Next
To upload supplier contact details or assign your suppliers to an existing group, click Advanced.Next
Upload contact information for your suppliers by entering a first and last name, along with a valid email. If unavailable, use “Valued Supplier” for the first name and “,” for the last name.Next
After completing the initial upload, a list of suppliers will appear, along with any flagged errors. At this stage, you can correct errors or check if a supplier already exists on the platform.Next
After correcting all errors in your supplier import, click Summary Report to extract a data summary.Next
When all errors have been addressed, click on Import to upload the suppliers to the platform.Next
Click on Fields to customise which columns of information appear.Next
To add suppliers to an existing group, select the Import to existing group dropdown and choose from the list. If not selected, a new group will be created with the import date and timestamp.Next
After importing your suppliers, they’ll appear in the list, and you can search for them using the search function.Next
Sort allows you to organise the supplier table based on your preferences.Next
Download lets you save a CSV report of the current supplier table.Next
Refresh updates the supplier table with the latest information.Next

Thank you for completing this guide.If you require assistance, please reach out to your Account Manager via email or the Customer Support Team at support@informed365.com.Go to Supplier Engagement (Tab 2)
Welcome to the Modern Slavery ModuleSupplier Engagement manages bulk communications and tracks engagement trendsContinue
Click on Supplier Engagement to view suppliers at different stages.Next
Engaged is the number of suppliers that have been invited or have an active primary contact and a Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) in their supplier profile.Next
Engagement Rates is the percentage of suppliers engaged out of the total number of suppliers invited who have accepted their invitation to the Informed 365 platform.Next

Not Engaged = Suppliers that have not been invited to the application.Queued = Suppliers that have been invited whose invitation email is queued to be sent.Invited = Suppliers whose invitation email has moved from Queued and has been sent to the recipient.Started = Suppliers that have been invited who have started working on their questionnaire.Completed = Suppliers that have completed and submitted their questionnaire.Next
The Engagement Status pie chart displays the breakdown of the statuses from the previous step.Next
The Engagement Trend graph will visually display the breakdown of the various statuses defined in the previous step and will also display a comparison between time periods.Next
Import Contacts will allow you to add an existing supplier profile that you wish to invite to the platform to complete an SAQ.Next
Click on Add Record to add an existing supplier's record whom you would like to invite to the application complete an SAQ.Next
Click on Add File to add multiple existing supplier profiles whom you would like to invite to the application to complete an SAQ.Next
Enter the supplier's name, contact first name, contact last name and contact email address.Next

Once the supplier information has been added to the fields below, the Import button will light up. Click to add the contact details to the relevant supplier records under My Suppliers or Other Suppliers.Next

classification.summary: This 40-step demo is a procedural walkthrough of the Modern Slavery Module's three tabs (Inherent Risk, Supplier Engagement, Reports), clicking through UI elements with minimal narrative structure. Each step explains a single button, field, or metric in isolation without connecting features to a meaningful business outcome or addressing who needs this value and why they should believe in it.
strengths: Comprehensive coverage of all features and workflows
Step-by-step clarity on how to perform specific tasks (add suppliers, upload files, filter data)
Organized into three logical chapters by feature area
Functional descriptions of terminology (ISRI rating, engagement status definitions)
weaknesses: No overarching narrative — feels like a feature dump with no red thread connecting steps to a single outcome
Generic one-size-fits-all messaging with no persona targeting (compliance officer? procurement? risk manager?)
Zero credibility/proof elements — no metrics, customer logos, before/after comparisons, or specific business results
Excessive length (40 steps) for what could be condensed into 5-10 steps if organized by outcome
Copy is purely functional and transactional ('Click on this tab', 'Enter the company name') with no emotional resonance or acknowledgement of user pain
No hook or opening problem statement — assumes viewers already know why they need Modern Slavery compliance
Missing specific value prop: what does importing suppliers actually prevent or enable?

Middle: 27 steps of procedural UI navigation across Inherent Risk (add/upload/filter suppliers) and Supplier Engagement (manage communications/track status). classification.proof_elements: None. No customer logos, testimonials, metrics, data comparisons, or specific business impact claims. Definitions and feature descriptions are provided but no evidence that this approach works or delivers measurable value.
classification.recommendation: Restructure the demo around a single buyer persona (e.g., 'Compliance Manager reducing supplier risk assessment time by 60%') and one clear outcome (e.g., 'Assess and onboard 500+ suppliers in 4 weeks instead of 16'). Collapse 40 steps into 8-10 steps organized by outcome, not feature. Add a credibility layer: include a specific metric (time saved, risk reduction percentage, or compliance coverage), a customer logo or testimonial snippet, and a before/after comparison. Open with a hook that speaks to their specific pain (e.g., 'Manual supplier vetting takes months and leaves blind spots'). Remove procedural detail and use voiceover to guide the story arc instead of step-by-step narration.

classification.summary: Advent Calendar by Channable delivers a role-based interactive demo with three distinct personas (Brand Owner, Marketplace Specialist, PPC Specialist), each with a clear narrative arc connecting features to a single outcome: scaling e-commerce operations with less manual effort. The demo effectively uses persona branching to tailor messaging and workflows to specific job roles and pain points.
strengths: Exceptional persona targeting: Three completely separate journeys (Brand Owner, Marketplace Specialist, PPC Specialist) with tailored pain points and outcomes for each role
Clear value proposition tied to outcomes: Each persona journey connects features to a single long-term benefit (scale + automation + centralization)
Logical feature progression: Features ladder meaningfully from setup/import → listing/connection → optimization → automation → insights
Appropriate demo length: 7-9 steps per persona avoids cognitive overload while providing sufficient detail
Strong emotional connection: Copy acknowledges specific role challenges ('busy teams', 'manual builds', 'listing rejections') and speaks directly to success metrics
Concrete feature specificity: Mentions real integrations (Amazon, eBay, bol, Google, Meta), specific capabilities (RSA/DSA templates, Quality tab, Order Connections), and tangible benefits
Red thread consistency: All three personas emphasize time savings + automation + scale as core value promise
weaknesses: Credibility score lower: Demo lacks specific proof elements — no customer logos, testimonials, before/after metrics, real performance numbers, or case studies to validate claims
No quantified outcomes: Missing specific metrics like 'saves X hours/week', 'reduces errors by X%', or 'increases listings by X%' — relies on aspirational language instead
Generic closing CTAs: All three personas end with identical 'Ready to [benefit]? Try Channable' messaging rather than role-specific conversion incentives
No social proof: No customer testimonials, case studies, or user counts to build trust and credibility
Limited visual differentiation: While copy is persona-tailored, no indication of visual design changes or role-specific color schemes to reinforce persona identity
Missing competitor positioning: No mention of why Channable is better than alternatives or what makes it uniquely suited to each role
classification.narrative_arc: Opening: Each persona receives a tailored hook acknowledging their specific challenge (centralization for brand owners, listing optimization for specialists, campaign automation for PPC). Middle: Features progress logically from data import/setup → marketplace/channel connections → template automation → performance tracking → dynamic rule-based optimization. Closing: All three journeys converge on the promise of scaling with less manual effort, ending with CTA to try the platform.
classification.persona_targeting: Highly targeted to three distinct roles: (1) Brand Owner/eCommerce Manager — focused on centralization, multi-channel coordination, and reducing tool fatigue; (2) Marketplace Specialist — focused on listing optimization, data quality, and preventing rejections; (3) PPC Specialist — focused on campaign automation, performance segmentation, and scaling at speed. Each persona receives a completely separate 7-9 step journey with role-specific language and feature priorities.
classification.proof_elements: Minimal credibility support. Demo mentions real platform integrations (Amazon, Google, eBay, bol, Meta) and specific features (Quality tab, RSA/DSA, Order Connections add-on), but includes no customer logos, testimonials, before/after comparisons, specific performance metrics, case studies, or user counts. Copy uses aspirational language ('save hours every week', 'prevent rejected listings') without quantified evidence.
classification.recommendation: Add credibility layer: Embed 2-3 customer success metrics or mini-case studies at the closing screen of each persona journey (e.g., 'See how Brand X reduced feed errors by 85% and scaled to 12 marketplaces' or 'Marketplace Specialist Y manages 50K+ listings with 60% less manual work'). Include specific before/after numbers and customer logos to anchor claims with evidence. This would elevate the demo from 8/10 to 9-10/10 by closing the credibility gap.
